name: Daniel Whitaker
title: Bridge Asset Manager
bio: Daniel oversees the health of Colorado’s state-owned bridges, turning inspection data into long-term investment plans.
favoritePart: Knowing the bridges I help maintain carry millions of people safely every year.
advice: Learn to read a bridge like a story — every crack and joint is telling you something.
funFact: Has photographed over 300 Colorado bridges for fun.

name: Priya Nair
title: Senior Bridge Engineer
bio: Priya designs and rehabilitates bridge structures across the I-70 mountain corridor.
favoriteProject: Floyd Hill to Veterans Memorial Tunnels Improvements
favoritePart: The moment a design leaves the screen and becomes steel and concrete.
advice: Master the fundamentals of statics — everything else builds on them.
funFact: Plays cello in a community orchestra.

name: Marcus Bell
title: Bridge Inspection Lead
bio: Marcus leads the crews that climb, rappel, and rate bridges in every corner of the state.
favoriteProject: Colorado Bridge Inspection & Load Rating Initiative
favoritePart: Working outdoors and solving puzzles that keep people safe.
advice: Get into the field early; you learn more under a bridge than in any classroom.
funFact: Certified rope-access technician and avid rock climber.

name: Hannah Brooks
title: Bridge Engineer I
bio: Hannah is an early-career structural engineer focused on bridge load ratings and rehab design.
favoriteProject: South Platte River Bridge Scour Countermeasures
favoritePart: Learning something new from senior engineers almost every day.
advice: Ask questions relentlessly — curiosity is a skill.
funFact: Builds intricate model bridges out of popsicle sticks.

name: Elena Vargas
title: Hydraulics Program Manager
bio: Elena leads CDOT’s hydraulics program, protecting roads and bridges from Colorado’s flash floods.
favoritePart: Designing infrastructure that stands up to nature at its most extreme.
advice: Respect water — it always finds the weakness in your design.
funFact: Whitewater kayaks the rivers she helps model.

name: Tom Castellano
title: Senior Hydraulics Engineer
bio: Tom models rivers and designs the culverts and bridges that cross them.
favoriteProject: I-70 Glenwood Canyon Resiliency & Rockfall Mitigation
favoritePart: Seeing a culvert I designed handle a 100-year storm without a hitch.
advice: Field-verify everything; the real world rarely matches the plan set.

name: Aisha Rahman
title: Water Resources Engineer
bio: Aisha specializes in bridge scour and floodplain analysis for major river crossings.
favoriteProject: South Platte River Bridge Scour Countermeasures
favoritePart: The detective work of figuring out how water moves around a structure.
advice: Learn GIS early — spatial thinking sets great water engineers apart.

name: Kevin O’Donnell
title: Hydraulics Engineer I
bio: Kevin supports drainage design and floodplain studies across the Front Range.
favoritePart: Turning messy field data into a clean, working model.
advice: Double-check your units — hydrology is unforgiving.
funFact: Storm-chases supercells across the eastern plains.

name: Robert Tanaka
title: Resident Engineer
bio: Robert runs major construction projects in the field, from groundbreaking to ribbon-cutting.
favoriteProject: I-25 South Gap Widening (Monument to Castle Rock)
favoritePart: Standing on a finished interchange that started as lines on paper.
advice: Build relationships with your contractors — trust moves projects.
funFact: Builds his own furniture in a home wood shop.

name: Sofia Mendez
title: Construction Project Engineer
bio: Sofia manages budgets, schedules, and quality on Front Range construction projects.
favoritePart: Solving the daily puzzle of keeping a project on time and on budget.
advice: Document everything — your notes are your best memory and defense.

name: James Okoro
title: Construction Inspector
bio: James verifies that what gets built matches the plans and specs, one pour at a time.
favoriteProject: North I-25 Express Lanes (Berthoud to Fort Collins)
favoritePart: Catching the small things before they become big problems.
advice: Learn to read plans fluently — it is the language of the field.

name: Megan Foster
title: Construction Engineer I
bio: Megan supports field engineering and project controls on rural highway projects.
favoritePart: Being outside and seeing tangible progress every single day.
advice: Say yes to field assignments — they accelerate your learning.

name: David Kim
title: Transportation Planning Manager
bio: David leads long-range and corridor planning that shapes Colorado’s transportation future.
favoriteProject: Front Range Passenger Rail Planning Study
favoritePart: Connecting community voices to real infrastructure decisions.
advice: Planning is half data, half listening — get good at both.

name: Rachel Green
title: Senior Transportation Planner
bio: Rachel turns travel data into corridor plans and multimodal strategies.
favoritePart: Finding the story hidden in a messy dataset.
advice: Learn to visualize data — a good map wins more arguments than a spreadsheet.

name: Anthony Russo
title: Multimodal Planner
bio: Anthony plans bike, pedestrian, and transit improvements across mountain communities.
favoritePart: Designing streets that work for people, not just cars.
advice: Spend time in the places you plan for — context is everything.

name: Linda Tran
title: Planning Analyst I
bio: Linda supports data analysis and public engagement for statewide planning studies.
favoriteProject: Front Range Passenger Rail Planning Study
favoritePart: Helping people understand complex projects through clear visuals.
advice: Get comfortable with ambiguity — planning rarely has one right answer.
funFact: Hand-letters event posters as a hobby.

name: Sarah Chen
title: Environmental Program Manager
bio: Sarah guides projects through environmental review, balancing infrastructure and ecology.
favoritePart: Finding solutions that move projects forward and protect Colorado’s landscapes.
advice: Get involved early — environmental issues are cheapest to solve at the start.

name: Michael Berg
title: NEPA Specialist
bio: Michael prepares the environmental documents that keep projects compliant and on schedule.
favoriteProject: Vail Pass Auxiliary Lanes & Wildlife Crossings
favoritePart: Untangling regulations so engineers can build.
advice: Writing is an engineering skill — learn to make complex things clear.

name: Gabriela Santos
title: Wildlife Biologist
bio: Gabriela designs wildlife crossings and connectivity solutions for highway projects.
favoritePart: Watching trail-cam footage of animals using a crossing we designed.
advice: Pair your science with engineering literacy — you will be unstoppable.

name: Eric Larsson
title: Environmental Engineer I
bio: Eric supports stormwater compliance and environmental permitting statewide.
favoritePart: Doing work that visibly protects rivers and wildlife.
advice: Learn the permits cold — they shape every project schedule.
funFact: Competitive birder with a 300-species life list.

name: Frank DeLuca
title: Maintenance Superintendent
bio: Frank leads the crews that keep mountain highways open through Colorado winters.
favoriteProject: I-70 Glenwood Canyon Resiliency & Rockfall Mitigation
favoritePart: Knowing my team keeps people moving safely in brutal conditions.
advice: Respect the people in the field — they know things no manual teaches.

name: Nicole Adams
title: Maintenance Asset Manager
bio: Nicole uses data to decide where every maintenance dollar does the most good.
favoritePart: Turning a flood of asset data into a clear plan of action.
advice: Data tells you where; the field tells you why — use both.

name: Carlos Jimenez
title: Avalanche Mitigation Specialist
bio: Carlos forecasts and mitigates avalanche hazards above Colorado’s mountain passes.
favoritePart: Reading the snowpack and making the call that keeps a pass safe.
advice: In the mountains, humility is a survival skill.

name: Tasha Williams
title: Maintenance Technician II
bio: Tasha keeps the eastern plains highways plowed, patched, and safe.
favoritePart: The variety — no two days on the road are the same.
advice: Master your equipment; it is your most important teammate.

name: Brian Schultz
title: Traffic & Safety Program Manager
bio: Brian leads data-driven safety programs aimed at reducing serious crashes statewide.
favoriteProject: Rural Highway Safety & Rumble Strip Deployment
favoritePart: Knowing our work measurably saves lives.
advice: Let the crash data, not assumptions, point you to the fix.

name: Jasmine Carter
title: Traffic Operations Engineer
bio: Jasmine designs and retimes traffic signals to keep corridors flowing.
favoritePart: Watching a corridor I retimed clear up at rush hour.
advice: Simulate before you build — models catch expensive mistakes.

name: Derek Nguyen
title: ITS Engineer
bio: Derek deploys intelligent transportation systems on Colorado’s busiest corridors.
favoriteProject: I-70 Mountain Corridor Variable Speed Limits
favoritePart: Bringing new technology to roads built decades ago.
advice: Stay curious about tech — this field reinvents itself constantly.

name: Olivia Park
title: Safety Analyst I
bio: Olivia analyzes crash patterns to prioritize the state’s next safety improvements.
favoritePart: Finding the pattern that explains a dangerous intersection.
advice: Statistics is a superpower — invest in it.
funFact: Plays on a competitive trivia team.

name: Alan Pierce
title: Applied Research Manager
bio: Alan runs CDOT’s research program, piloting the innovations that become tomorrow’s standards.
favoritePart: Testing bold ideas before anyone else gets to.
advice: Be rigorous and brave — good research needs both.
funFact: Holds two patents in sensor technology.

name: Maya Patel
title: Data Scientist
bio: Maya builds the models and dashboards that turn CDOT’s data into decisions.
favoritePart: The first time a model predicts something real and gets it right.
advice: Learn to tell stories with data — models mean nothing if no one acts on them.

name: Jordan Mitchell
title: Research Engineer
bio: Jordan investigates new pavement materials and construction methods in the field and lab.
favoritePart: Bridging the gap between a lab result and a real road.
advice: Question the standard — every spec was once an experiment.
funFact: Home-roasts coffee and logs every batch as data.

name: Emily Zhao
title: Research Analyst I
bio: Emily supports research projects with data analysis, visualization, and literature review.
favoritePart: Getting to learn a little about every part of CDOT.
advice: Build a portfolio of small projects — proof beats promises.

name: Greg Halvorsen
title: Geotechnical Program Manager
bio: Greg leads the geotechnical team tackling Colorado’s landslides, rockfall, and foundations.
favoriteProject: Wolf Creek Pass Geohazard & Avalanche Mitigation
favoritePart: Outsmarting a mountain that does not want a road on it.
advice: Get your boots muddy — geotech is learned in the field.
funFact: Amateur geologist who hunts for fossils.

name: Diana Flores
title: Pavement Materials Engineer
bio: Diana designs pavements and tests the materials that make Colorado’s roads last.
favoritePart: Knowing the mix designs I approve will ride smooth for decades.
advice: Sweat the details — materials fail at the margins.

name: Sam Whitehorse
title: Geotechnical Engineer
bio: Sam investigates soils and slopes for bridges, walls, and mountain corridors.
favoritePart: Drilling into a slope and finally understanding what is going on underground.
advice: Learn to log a boring well — it is the foundation of everything geotech.

name: Beth Coleman
title: Materials Lab Technician II
bio: Beth runs the lab tests that decide whether materials make it onto Colorado’s roads.
favoritePart: The precision — good testing is part science, part craft.
advice: Respect the procedure; consistency is what makes data trustworthy.
funFact: Pottery hobbyist (also a kind of materials testing).

name: Joan Reyes
title: Aeronautics Division Manager
bio: Joan leads Colorado’s aeronautics division, supporting the state’s 70+ public-use airports.
favoritePart: Helping small mountain communities stay connected by air.
advice: See the whole system — aviation is a network, not a list of airports.

name: Patrick Mulhern
title: Airport Engineer
bio: Patrick designs runway, taxiway, and drainage improvements for airports across the Western Slope.
favoritePart: Designing pavement that handles both jets and harsh mountain weather.
advice: Learn the FAA standards deeply — they govern everything you design.

name: Wendy Cho
title: Aviation Planner
bio: Wendy develops airport layout plans and statewide aviation studies.
favoritePart: Mapping out how an airport will grow over the next 20 years.
advice: Relationships matter — aviation is a small, connected world.
funFact: Plane-spotter with a photo log of 200+ aircraft.

name: Tyler Brooks
title: Aviation Engineer I
bio: Tyler supports airport pavement design and FAA grant projects statewide.
favoritePart: Getting to work on something that literally helps people take flight.
advice: Be the person who follows through — reliability gets you noticed.
funFact: Building a flight simulator in his garage.

name: Sonia Irakoze
title: Summer Engineering Intern
bio: Sonia is a summer engineering intern who designed and built CDOT Compass for the Innovation Challenge — a prototype that helps interns discover the opportunities that already exist across the department.
favoritePart: Turning a real intern frustration into something that could actually help the next group of interns.
advice: Be curious. Reach out to people outside your own team — you’ll be surprised how much you can learn.
funFact: This spotlight was created inside the app it describes.
